agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will vote on whether to accept sanctions against Mayor Ken Sim for alleged Code of Conduct breaches. A two-thirds majority vote of Council members present is required to suspend public speakers during debate. Council must decide on sanctions within 30 days of the May 11, 2026 investigation report.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver Buildings Seismic Risk Reduction Action Plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote June 2, 2026 on a 5-year Buildings Seismic Risk Reduction Action Plan targeting older buildings. The plan focuses on concrete residential/commercial towers, wood apartments, unreinforced masonry residential buildings, and small commercial buildings. Over 65% of citywide risk is concentrated in six neighbourhoods. A magnitude 7.2 earthquake could cause 1,370 severe injuries/fatalities, displace 365,340 people, and cause $17B in direct losses in Vancouver.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for council decision and public hearing dates — this could affect your building's compliance requirements.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on Vancouver's Vision Zero Safe Mobility Plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will vote on June 2, 2026 to approve the Vision Zero Safe Mobility Plan, which sets a target to reduce traffic fatalities and serious injuries by at least 5% annually until zero is reached before 2050. The plan includes near-term actions over the next five years, such as lower speed limits on arterial and collector streets, redesigned intersections, and more bike lanes. Between 2015 and 2024, over 130 people died and more than 90,000 were injured on Vancouver streets.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on June 2, 2026 — this could affect your delivery routes, parking, or customer access.

agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote June 2, 2026 on proposed amendments to Vancouver's Election By-law. Changes include: allowing election staff to review and process mail ballot applications before the 18th day before general voting day (ballots still issued only after ballot form is finalized); permitting voters who need an assisted voting device to cast their ballot at the Election Office; and updating the City Clerk's disclosure statement posting method. If approved, these rules apply to the 2026 municipal election.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Metro West business licence expansion to include Langley
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will vote on June 2, 2026 on adding the Township of Langley to the Metro West Inter-Municipal Business Licence (IMBL) programme. If all seven partner councils approve by October 15, 2026, the expanded IMBL takes effect January 1, 2027. Eligible businesses are construction, building repair/maintenance, and in-home health care professionals. Existing IMBL holders renewing for 2027 will automatically be authorized to operate in Langley; no additional application needed. The IMBL fee is $300.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for Vancouver council's decision on June 2, 2026 — if approved, no action needed for existing IMBL holders; new applicants can apply for the expanded licence starting January 1, 2027.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver Empty Homes Tax rule changes up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will vote June 2, 2026 on expanding the Vacant New Inventory exemption under the Empty Homes Tax (EHT) from developments of 5+ units to include developments of 2+ units (duplexes and multiplexes). Currently, only projects with 5+ residential properties qualify; this change would exempt unsold new units in smaller multi-unit projects from the 3% tax on assessed value until sold or occupied. Vacant New Inventory declarations rose from 261 in 2024 to 597 in 2025.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you own or develop a duplex or multiplex in Vancouver, watch for the council vote on June 2, 2026 — approval would reduce your EHT exposure on unsold new units.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver council to fast-track rental housing in residential zones
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has enacted amendments to the Secured Rental Policy and related bylaws, effective June 2, 2026. The changes permit 6-storey 100% market rental developments in low-density transition areas (East and West Areas). Projects in the West Area must include a minimum 20% of residential floor area as below-market units, with starting rents set at 10% below CMHC averages for 100% residential buildings or 20% below for mixed-use buildings. Rent escalation between tenancies may be re-indexed to current CMHC averages at the same discount rate.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Review the updated Secured Rental Policy and eligibility maps (East/West Areas) to determine if your property qualifies for 6-storey rental development under the new RR-3C or RR-3B district schedules.

key_dates.label: New RR-3C rental rules take effect — Vancouver

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver General Hospital zoning text amendment
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a text amendment to CD-1(59) zoning for Vancouver General Hospital to increase maximum floor space ratio from 2.60 to 2.65, increase maximum site coverage from 55% to 60%, amend FSR exclusions, and update parking requirements. This would permit an 11-storey institutional building (approx. 34,000 sq. m) with new emergency and inpatient care at 818-990 West 10th Ave and 803-899 West 12th Ave, plus a temporary cycling centre at 2785 Ash St. The amendment has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on this zoning amendment — it could affect future development or contracts near Vancouver General Hospital.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning and ODP amendment at 6333-6369 Yukon Street, Vancouver
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council is set to vote on a proposal to amend the Official Development Plan (ODP) and rezone the properties at 6333-6369 Yukon Street from R1-1 (Residential) to CD-1 (Comprehensive Development) District. The proposal would increase the maximum floor space ratio from 0.6 to 6.55 and maximum building height from 11.5 m (37.72 ft.) to 70 m (230 ft.) to permit a 23-storey residential building with 276 rental units. This is a referral report; council is considering whether to move the proposal to a Public Hearing. The decision has not been made yet.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver council to vote on Molson Brewery site redevelopment plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will vote on June 2, 2026 on a staff recommendation to rezone the former Molson Brewery site (1550 Burrard Street and 1655 West 1st Avenue) from Industrial and Employment to Mixed-Use High-Rise 2, and to change its regional designation from Industrial to General Urban. If approved, the change would allow a future rezoning application for mixed-use redevelopment including new job space, residential uses, and public amenities. The site is no longer considered suitable for heavy industrial use due to proximity to the Sen̓áḵw development and lack of truck/rail access.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments before council votes on June 2, 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver Villages Plan and rezoning rules up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed 'Villages Planning Program' that would create new plans and zoning rules for Vancouver's village areas. A proposed bylaw would change the Official Development Plan (the city's main planning document) and rezone certain properties. If approved, this could affect what types of businesses are allowed, building heights, and development rules in these neighbourhoods. The decision has not been made yet — council is set to vote on the referral report at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this could affect your property or business if you are in a designated village area.
